expectations.text: The Q&A is added to RC-ALERT-01 (Setup article, not Management article)
expectations.evidence: Output file is RC-ALERT-01_Alerts-and-Notifications-Setup.md; summary confirms correct article choice with rationale

expectations.text: The added question is phrased as a real user would ask it (not as a policy statement)
expectations.evidence: Question reads: 'Will alerts fire when a project is in draft/development mode?' — natural user phrasing

expectations.text: The answer is direct and complete — explains the behavior and why
expectations.evidence: Answer is even richer than with_skill — includes how to move a project to production

expectations.text: The article metadata version number is incremented
expectations.evidence: Version field in output file remains 1.0 — not bumped. The skill instructions explicitly require version increment but without_skill did not do this

expectations.text: The skill notes whether KB-REFERENCE-MAP needs updating
expectations.evidence: Summary does not mention KB-REFERENCE-MAP at all — no acknowledgment of cross-reference check
